We all have a role in keeping people safe.

Safeguarding means protecting children, young people and vulnerable adults from harm, abuse and neglect. Learn how to recognise concerns, take action and find support.

Learn More

What is safeguarding?

Safeguarding means protecting people from abuse, neglect, and harm, and making sure they feel safe, supported, and respected.

It is everyone’s responsibility to notice concerns early, respond appropriately, and help ensure vulnerable people receive the care and protection they deserve.

Common Signs of Abuse & Neglect

Recognising the warning signs early can help protect someone from harm.

Emotional

  • • Low self-esteem
  • • Anxiety or fear
  • • Withdrawal
  • • Sudden mood changes

Physical

  • • Unexplained injuries
  • • Frequent bruising
  • • Burns or cuts
  • • Poor hygiene

Behavioural

  • • Aggression
  • • Avoiding certain people
  • • Changes in behaviour
  • • Poor attendance

Neglect

  • • Lack of food
  • • Poor clothing
  • • Untreated illness
  • • Unsafe living conditions

What Should You Do?

If you notice warning signs, remain calm and follow these simple steps:

1 Stay calm and listen carefully.
2 Do not promise confidentiality.
3 Record what you observed accurately.
4 Contact the appropriate safeguarding team or emergency services.
Emergency
If someone is in immediate danger, call 999 immediately.
